digest.top_line: NIVOPOSTOP: 3y DFS ~63% vs 53% adding adjuvant nivo to post-op cisplatin-CRT; KEYNOTE-689 EFS-positive perioperative pembro, both layering IO around an unchanged (chemo)RT backbone.
digest.tldr: Two positive IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC, but for the radiation oncologist neither touches RT: dose, fractionation, and target volume are unchanged, with IO layered before/after the curative (chemo)RT course. NIVOPOSTOP targets the classic high-risk adjuvant group (ENE, +margins, ≥4 nodes); both still abstract/EFS-stage with DFS/EFS HRs not in source.

sites.intro: IO is moving into resectable HNSCC around an unchanged curative (chemo)RT backbone, so the RT decision stays fixed while only the systemic layer changes.

studies.name: KEYNOTE-689 vs NIVOPOSTOP
studies.tldr: Two positive IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC: NIVOPOSTOP 3y DFS ~63% vs 53% (adjuvant nivo), KEYNOTE-689 EFS-positive (perioperative pembro).
details: 🔍 Educational round-up (curator infographic) contrasting two IO trials in resectable/resected LA-HNSCC, not new primary data
📊 NIVOPOSTOP: 3-yr DFS ~63% vs 53% adding nivo to post-op cisplatin-CRT vs CRT alone (ASCO 2025)
📊 KEYNOTE-689: significant EFS benefit, first positive perioperative IO trial in resectable HNSCC in >2 decades; no effect size reported in source tweets
🔍 Both keep (chemo)RT as the curative backbone; neither changes RT dose, fractionation, or target volume, IO is layered around the RT course
🔍 NIVOPOSTOP enrolls the classic adjuvant-CRT high-risk group (ENE, positive margins, ≥4 nodes, extensive PNI) per Bernier/Cooper indication
🔍 Infographic suggests greater benefit in PD-L1/CPS-high tumors; no cutoff or subgroup effect size given in source
⚠️ NIVOPOSTOP is ASCO 2025 abstract-stage (DFS), not yet full peer-reviewed publication; OS not reported in source
⚠️ KEYNOTE-689 EFS magnitude (HR/median) and NIVOPOSTOP DFS HR not in source; numbers here limited to the 3y DFS % pair
studies.significance: Neither trial changes the RT itself: adjuvant (chemo)RT stays the curative backbone and IO is layered around it. NIVOPOSTOP adds nivo to post-op cisplatin-CRT in the classic ENE/positive-margin high-risk group (3y DFS ~63% vs 53%); KEYNOTE-689 shifts IO to neoadjuvant. The move is whether to add peri-RT IO, not to alter dose or volume.
studies.monday_clinic: In resectable stage III-IVA HNSCC, this informs whether to add IO around the surgery-plus-(chemo)RT course, neoadjuvant pembrolizumab up front or adjuvant nivolumab for ENE/positive-margin pathology; it does not extend to unresectable or definitive-CRT-only disease.
open_questions: Neoadjuvant vs adjuvant IO sequencing in resectable HNSCC
Does IO benefit concentrate in PD-L1/CPS-high tumors
NIVOPOSTOP overall survival and peer-reviewed results

related_trials.brief_title: Phase II Multi-centered Study of Perioperative Ivonescimab Versus Pembrolizumab Combined with Standard of Care (SOC) in Patients with Resectable, Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.brief_summary:
Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) is one of the common malignant tumors in the world. More than 60% of HNSCC patients are locally advanced when first diagnosed. The treatment effect of locally advanced HNSCC is not ideal. About 50-60% of patients will have local recurrence within 2 years, and 20-30% of patients will have distant metastasis. The 5-year disease control rate is approximately 40%, and the 5-year overall survival rate is less than 50%.

In recent years, PD-1 inhibitors…

related_trials.brief_title: Neoadjuvant Toripalimab + Chemotherapy ± Cetuximab in Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (Neo-ICT)
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is a randomized, active-controlled, open-label clinical trial for participants with newly diagnosed Stage III-IVb, resectable, locoregionally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (LA-HNSCC). The study consists of two experimental arms and one control arm. Participants in Experimental Arm A will receive two cycles of Toripalimab, albumin-bound paclitaxel, carboplatin, and cetuximab prior to surgery. Participants in Experimental Arm B will receive two cycles of Toripalimab,…

related_trials.brief_title: Postoperative CCRT Followed by Immunotherapy in High-Risk LA HNSCC
related_trials.brief_summary: This trial aims to evaluate whether the addition of anti-PD-1 antibody to adjuvant postoperative chemoradiotherapy could improve disease free survival in patients with high-risk locally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC).
conditions: Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma

related_trials.brief_title: A Trial Evaluating the Addition of Nivolumab to Cisplatin-RT for Treatment of Cancers of the Head and Neck
related_trials.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the efficacy of nivolumab + cisplatin-RT relative to standard of care (SOC) cisplatin-RT alone, using the disease-free survival (DFS by investigator imaging assessment) as primary endpoint )
